stories.title: EastEnders airs horrific Joel Marshall attack twist in early iPlayer release
stories.summary: Joel Marshall faces brutal revenge from teens in EastEnders after returning to Walford. On Wednesday’s episode (22 July), Avani Nandra-Hart and Amy Mitchell lure him to a park party, where the group plans to confront him. Ross Marshall, Joel’s father, reluctantly allows his attendance, unaware of their intentions. The twist escalates tensions in Albert Square.

stories.title: Charli XCX recrute Vincent Cassel dans le clip de son nouveau single "Camera"
stories.summary: Charli XCX’s new single Camera drops July 24 with a noir-and-white clip featuring Vincent Cassel in a chaotic desert shoot. Cassel’s explosive scenes kept failing—first a misfired charge, then a flaming light—until he stormed off, mimicking his iconic La Haine pistol gesture. The track is the final single from her album Music, Fashion, Film.

stories.title: PRONOS PARIS RMC Le pari sûr du 22 juillet - Ligue Conférence
stories.summary: SC Braga faces FK Železničar Pančevo in the second qualifying round of the UEFA Europa Conference League on July 22 at 21:00 CET. Braga, unbeaten in five pre-season matches, is the clear favorite after opening their domestic league campaign with a win. The Portuguese side must travel to Sportsko-Rekreativni Centar Mladost in Pančevo, Serbia, for the first leg. Braga’s experience and squad depth are expected to secure a positive result ahead of the return fixture.

stories.title: Morant: "Feijóo está dibujando una coalición que se parece cada vez más a esos gobiernos criminales como el de Hitler"
stories.summary: Spain’s science minister Diana Morant accused PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo of steering a potential coalition toward policies resembling Hitler’s regime, citing rising xenophobic rhetoric and racist attacks on footballer Lamine Yamal. The PP demanded an apology and retraction, calling her comparison unacceptable. Morant warned of laws targeting citizens and urged the PP to condemn hate speech. A PP spokesperson called the remarks inflammatory.

stories.title: Meteo MARCHE Video: previsioni aggiornate
stories.summary: Heavy rain and localized thunderstorms hit the Marche region Friday, prompting a 36-hour yellow weather alert from 2 p.m. on July 11 to midnight on July 12. Intense downpours struck inland and high-hill areas, including Piandimeleto, Mercatale and Peglio in the Pesaro province. Brief coastal and hillside storms are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday, clearing by mid-morning. Conditions will then stabilize with dry, sunny weather through Monday.

stories.title: Islande: vague d'indignation dans le pays après le saccage d'un site naturel par un touriste français
stories.summary: Un touriste français a détruit un site naturel protégé en Islande en juin avec deux Land Rover, diffusant les dégâts sur les réseaux sociaux. Il a écopé d’une amende de 3 000 euros, mais a repris ses dégradations peu après. Une seconde interpellation a eu lieu peu après. Les autorités locales et les habitants dénoncent ces actes, soulignant l’importance de préserver les écosystèmes fragiles.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies withdrew from the World Matchplay Darts 2026 in Blackpool after suffering high blood pressure during his match against Ross Smith. The Scotsman led 2-1 before collapsing on stage, prompting medical attention and his exit. Play resumed with Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price among the remaining contenders. The tournament continues with quarter-final spots still up for grabs.

stories.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
stories.summary: Aston Villa have agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until 2027, with a conditional obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentina international has already completed a medical and pre-signed a four-year contract at Villa Park. Chelsea expect the buy clause to be triggered, valuing the deal at £43m. Villa have spent nearly £200m on sales this summer.

stories.title: Chicago P.D., la tredicesima stagione da stasera su Italia 1: le anticipazioni del 22 luglio
stories.summary: Chicago P.D. Season 13 premieres tonight, July 22, at 9:30 p.m. on Italia 1 with 21 new episodes. The gritty police drama follows the Windy City’s Intelligence Unit, led by Sergeant Hank Voight, as they tackle fresh threats after a brutal internal conflict left the team fractured. New undercover agent Eva Imani joins the squad, while old tensions simmer beneath the surface. The season picks up where Season 12 left off, with Voight rebuilding his decimated unit.

stories.title: Televisión Canaria presenta ‘Venezuela: un mes bajo los escombros’
stories.summary: Televisión Canaria presents a special report Thursday at 21:30 on the aftermath of Venezuela’s June 24 double earthquake. The disaster killed over 5,000 people, injured thousands, and destroyed or severely damaged nearly 18,000 homes. Focused on La Guaira and Caracas, the program marks one month since the quake amid ongoing humanitarian efforts. Survivors’ testimonies highlight the slow recovery in the worst-hit areas.

stories.title: Il processo di stasera a "Un giorno in pretura"
stories.summary: The final hearing of Giampiero Gualandi, 64, accused of killing Sofia Stefani, 33, concludes tonight in Bologna. The vigilessa was shot with her service pistol on May 16, 2024, at a local police station in Anzola dell’Emilia. The trial, featured on Rai 3’s Un giorno in pretura at 9:15 PM, centers on a stormy relationship marked by a BDSM contract. Prosecutors claim Gualandi acted to silence Stefani over fears she’d expose their affair.

stories.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
stories.summary: Samsung unveiled three new foldable devices at Galaxy Unpacked in London on July 2026. The lineup includes the first Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra with an 8-inch foldable display and a 4.1mm thin profile. The Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8 also debuted, featuring AI-enhanced multitasking and FlexWindow improvements. Availability and pricing details were not announced.

stories.title: Restablecida la circulación de Cercanías en Madrid entre Getafe y Parla tras un incendio junto a la vía
stories.summary: Train service on Madrid’s Cercanías line C4 between Getafe Centro and Parla was suspended for about 30 minutes Wednesday after a fire near the tracks. Service resumed at 5:20 p.m. as emergency crews cleared the area. Delays and altered routes affected passengers before normal frequencies gradually returned.

stories.title: Death of teen in crash as garda pursued ruled accidental
stories.summary: A coroner ruled the 2019 death of 14-year-old Thomas Healy accidental after his car crashed while being pursued by Gardaí in Killarney. The teen lost control on Ross Road, striking a tree; his passenger, 15-year-old Alfie Barrett, survived with severe injuries. Garda Seán O’Sullivan testified he lost sight of the vehicle before the crash, and a 2022 probe cleared him of wrongdoing. The case highlights risks of high-speed pursuits involving young drivers.

stories.title: Gratteri bacchetta Salvini sul caso Roggero: “La politica si dia una calmata e la smetta di aizzare la gente”. Su La7
stories.summary: Procuratore capo di Napoli Nicola Gratteri ha esortato la politica a evitare di strumentalizzare casi giudiziari, citando le polemiche sugli scontri di Bologna e la condanna del gioielliere Mario Roggero. Gratteri ha sottolineato come entrambi i casi siano stati usati per propaganda elettorale, invitando a rispettare le sentenze definitive. Ha ribadito che la grazia può essere discussa solo dopo l’ingresso in carcere e con prove concrete di ravvedimento. Il magistrato ha chiuso ogni processo mediatico, affidando valutazioni ai soli educatori penitenziari.

stories.title: Africa’s most developed pharmaceutical market reviews 12 Ozempic generics as drugmakers target Novo Nordisk’s dominance
stories.summary: South Africa’s health regulator is reviewing 12 applications for generic versions of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic and Wegovy. The move follows the expiry of Novo Nordisk’s main patent in March 2026, opening the door for cheaper alternatives in one of Africa’s largest pharmaceutical markets. Analysts say the approvals could pressure Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly by expanding access to GLP-1 medicines used for diabetes and weight loss. Decisions on the generics are pending, with no timeline announced.

stories.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies’ World Matchplay Darts second-round match against Ross Smith was abandoned Wednesday night after he fell ill mid-game. The Scot was leading 2-1 when he became unsteady and required medical attention. Medics confirmed he was conscious and talking but did not return. Smith advances to the quarter-finals as a result.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
stories.summary: World Matchplay Darts 2026 runs from July 18 to July 22 in Blackpool, England. Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen are among the top seeds competing. Sky Sports will broadcast the tournament live across its platforms. The event concludes with the final on Wednesday, July 22.

stories.title: La Stazione spaziale internazionale attraversa i cieli: passaggio visibile anche nel Bresciano. Ecco quando
stories.summary: Tonight at 11:08 PM, the International Space Station will streak across the skies over Brescia, visible to the naked eye for about five minutes. The ISS will appear as a steady, bright light moving from northwest to east-northeast. Clear weather is needed for optimal viewing. Check Heavens-Above for precise local tracking details.

stories.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
stories.summary: A prolonged heatwave continues to affect the Languedoc region, with temperatures exceeding 30 degrees Celsius. According to Météo France, the heatwave is expected to last until Saturday, with a potential change in conditions on Saturday. Temperatures are expected to remain high, with a maximum temperature of 35 degrees Celsius forecasted for the region. Residents can expect a return to more typical weather patterns by Sunday.

stories.title: „Online-Banking demnächst gesperrt”: Diese Nachrichten sollten Sie direkt an die Sparkasse senden
stories.summary: Betrüger senden Mails über angebliches Sicherheitsupdate beim Sparkassen-Banking, um Empfänger auf gefälschte Seiten zu lotsen und Bankdaten abzugreifen. Die Nachricht droht mit einer Sperre des Online-Bankings, falls die angebliche Freischaltung nicht durchgeführt wird. Die Mail ist unpersönlich und der Absender nutzt eine Domain namens ashoj.com. Sparkassen-Kunden sollen sensible Daten preisgeben, indem sie auf eine gefälschte ChipTAN-Seite klicken.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted to $1,549.99 at Amazon and $1,549 at Best Buy, down from its usual $1,699 price. This premium iPad features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. Additionally, the 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage and optional 5G is marked down to $749, a $500 discount. Consumers can now purchase these devices at lower prices.

stories.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
stories.summary: Feyenoord's training camp in Belgium features a 32-player squad, including Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn, who have been working out extensively to improve their physique. Van Persie, recovering from a knee injury, aims to become the team's first striker and score many goals. He will need to impress the new coach, Giovanni van Bronckhorst, to achieve his goal. Van Persie's focus remains on his recovery and upcoming season.

stories.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
stories.summary: EastEnders has introduced a new character, Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr, who strikes up a friendship with Linda Carter. The two share an instant spark during a salsa dancing lesson at the community centre. Clive's arrival helps Linda in her quest to save the community centre, which is at risk of shutting down. As a result, Elaine decides to play matchmaker for the pair.

stories.title: ‘Disney Worldbuilders’ Documentary by Leslie Iwerks, Featuring James Cameron, Kevin Feige and Jon Favreau, Set to Premiere on Disney+
stories.summary: A new documentary, "Disney Worldbuilders," is set to premiere on Disney+ on August 20. The film, directed by Leslie Iwerks, takes viewers behind the scenes of Disney's immersive park experiences, including franchises like "Frozen" and "Zootopia." Featuring interviews with James Cameron, Kevin Feige, and Jon Favreau, the documentary showcases the creation of Disney's park worlds. The premiere marks a unique look at the intersection of film and theme park design.

stories.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing they can "weirdly take you out of reality." This ban was evident on his recent film "The Odyssey," where actors wore traditional Ancient Greek attire. Nolan's reasoning is that Uggs are a "signifier of modernity" that can detract from the film's historical setting. The ban is likely to continue on future sets, ensuring a more immersive experience for audiences.

stories.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
stories.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals €1.5 billion will be allocated for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This move aims to allow workers to keep more of their pay. Total government expenditure is expected to rise by 5.9% to €125 billion in 2027. The increased spending and tax package will be closely monitored to ensure alignment with the government's Medium Term Framework.

stories.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
stories.summary: Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has sparked a political row by ordering a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison in September. Approximately 6,000 killers, rapists, and sex offenders are set to be freed on tags. The move comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential early release of his killers. The review aims to address concerns over public safety and the criminal justice system.

stories.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
stories.summary: President Trump's tariffs have reshaped America's trading relationship with the world, with imports from countries like China falling by nearly 40% since tariffs went into effect. As a result, manufacturers have sought low-cost factories closer to home, prompting a boom in trade between the United States and Mexico. Laredo, Texas, has been a major beneficiary, with roughly 40% of U.S.-Mexico trade passing through the city. The city's World Trade Bridge is now a key hub for Mexican products entering the US.

stories.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to introduce a new financing program for leasing iPhones, which could allow the company to restrict access to apps on devices with missed payments. Under the program, iPhones in "Restricted Mode" would lose access to nearly all apps, except for basic functions. The feature is part of iOS 27 beta, which is still in development. If implemented, it could impact users who miss payments on their financed devices.

stories.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
stories.summary: Bose has launched the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, a flagship wireless speaker in its reimagined Lifestyle Collection, priced at $299. The speaker is currently a bestseller on Best Buy and is available in two colorways. It can be expanded into a multi-room setup or a full home theater over time. The Lifestyle Ultra Speaker is part of a new lineup that includes a soundbar and subwoofer, available in an exclusive bundle on Best Buy for $1,699.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Braunau am Inn, Austria, has been converted into a police station, marking the end of a yearslong effort to prevent the site from becoming a Nazi attraction. The 17th century building, located at Salzburger Vorstadt 15, has undergone renovations for about three years. The police station is now operational, with a new exterior and windows. This move comes as Austria continues to grapple with its role in the Holocaust.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A Florida teenager, 15-year-old R.K.C., has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial and a desire to focus on his recovery and therapy. The lawsuit claimed Meta's platforms, including Instagram, were addictive and harmed children. R.K.C. had previously settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. Meta has defended itself against the lawsuit, stating the claims were baseless.

stories.title: Toter Wal vom Badestrand entfernt - Teile des Kopfes fehlten
stories.summary: A dead pig whale washed up on Norderney's beach has been removed. The carcass was found near a popular bar on Monday and was transported to a facility for disposal. According to Norderney's mayor, Frank Ulrichs, the decision to remove it was made because it was in the area of the public beaches. The whale's head was partially missing.

stories.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
stories.summary: Will Ferrell stars in a new Netflix show, "The Hawk," a golf comedy that revisits his broad, dated style of humor. The 59-year-old actor, a key figure in the 2000s comedy scene, has stuck to this approach despite the shift in popular taste. The show, released on Netflix, marks a departure from the more nuanced comedy of his peers. Ferrell's decision to revive his signature style has sparked questions about whether it's time for a comeback.

stories.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
stories.summary: Former tennis star Ana Ivanović has revealed she ended her career early due to mental health issues, in addition to physical injuries. The 38-year-old, who won the 2008 French Open and reached number one in the world, spoke about her struggles in a recent interview. Ivanović said she experienced severe panic attacks on court, which she had never publicly discussed before. Her decision to retire at 29 in 2016 now takes on a more complex context.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother-of-four Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Prosecutors say the woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She has now been formally charged, with the investigating judge to decide if there's enough evidence for a trial. The case was opened on May 7, 2025.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: Global oil prices surged to a 14-month high on Wednesday, driven by escalating tensions between the US and Iran. The US has threatened to deepen its war effort, including targeting the Houthis, and warned Iran that attacking any ship in the Strait of Hormuz would prompt a US bombing of a bridge or power plant in Tehran. The conflict has already led to a significant decline in shipping traffic in the region. The situation remains volatile, with the US continuing to attack Iran.

stories.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers for a record £117m fee from Villa on Tuesday. Garnacho's loan is expected to be triggered if he meets certain performance conditions next season.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning follows 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran. Iran has warned of reprisals after Trump said the US would soon hit an underground nuclear site near Natanz.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: The UK's Conservative Party has asked HMRC to investigate a £5m gift to Nigel Farage, questioning whether it should be taxed as compensation for his failed bid to become an MP. The gift was given by crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne, who claims it was a personal and unconditional donation. Farage has previously stated the money was for personal security costs and a Brexit campaign reward. HMRC will now review the matter to determine if tax is owed.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility is the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years. The event will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and a media availability with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials. The wind tunnel is a major milestone in NASA's evolution, marking a significant step forward in the agency's research capabilities.

stories.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
stories.summary: Marvel Studios has officially canceled its Wonder Man Disney+ series, marking the end of an era for the character. The show was set to premiere in 2024, but production has been halted indefinitely. The decision comes after a year of development, with several cast members and crew members already attached to the project. The future of the character remains uncertain, but fans are left wondering what this means for the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

stories.title: « L’Agence : nouvelles destinations » est de retour sur TMC, que réserve la saison 2 ?
stories.summary: The reality TV show "L'Agence : nouvelles destinations" returns to TMC for its second season, with Martin and Valentin Kretz competing to find new destinations for their family's luxury real estate agency. The brothers, members of a family of high-end agents, aim to expand their business by discovering new markets. The competition, which drew nearly 500,000 viewers per episode in 2025, resumes on July 22, 2026. The stakes are high as the brothers vie for the best destination.

stories.title: RETE 4
stories.summary: A 91-year-old Illinois florist, Earl Stone, has turned to transporting large quantities of drugs to make ends meet after facing economic crisis. This marks a drastic change for the veteran war convert who once prioritized moral principles. The film, directed by Clint Eastwood, is based on a true story and explores themes of regret and redemption. As the film airs tonight at 9:35 PM, viewers can expect a poignant portrayal of an American icon's downfall.

stories.title: Fast Sylvester Stallone getötet: Dolph Lundgrens Reaktion auf den schlimmen Action-Vorfall bei „Rocky“
stories.summary: Sylvester Stallone was hospitalized in 1985 after Dolph Lundgren knocked him out during filming of Rocky IV. The incident occurred during a real boxing scene when Lundgren landed a powerful punch. Stallone suffered a concussion and required medical treatment. The film’s intense fight choreography reflected the era before advanced digital effects.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been handed a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell, kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes is due to serve the ban, with one game suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he was under investigation for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges. The investigation into his death and the ongoing probe into human trafficking charges will continue.

stories.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds
stories.summary: A new study reveals that 1,700 H.I.V. treatment sites worldwide have closed following the Trump administration's cuts to the largest global H.I.V. program last year. The cuts led to significant drops in prevention efforts and treatment for high-risk groups, including sex workers, men who have sex with men, and people who inject drugs. The study, conducted by the H.I.V. charity amfAR, found that three-quarters of affected organizations stopped providing services to these groups. The findings will be presented next week at an international AIDS conference in Rio de Janeiro.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation centers on discovery rights, an MLS regulation that gives a specific club first refusal over a player. Inter Miami acquired Casemiro's rights from the LA Galaxy, but the Galaxy held those rights initially. The league will review the case before making further comments.

stories.title: Dubai Firm Cuts Deal for New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
stories.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that ultra-processed foods are a primary driver of obesity and type 2 diabetes. The report suggests that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed, as evidence points to sugar and refined carbohydrates as the main culprits. The committee recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to hold them accountable for healthier food options. This could lead to a re-evaluation of food labelling and reformulation targets.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City amid a legionnaires' disease outbreak on Manhattan's Upper East Side, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings that tested positive for live legionella bacteria. Testing is ongoing, with results for 140 remaining cooling tower systems expected in the coming week. The city's health department will continue to monitor the situation.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.

offbeat.title: EastEnders airs horrific Joel Marshall attack twist in early iPlayer release
offbeat.summary: Joel Marshall faces brutal revenge from teens in EastEnders after returning to Walford. On Wednesday’s episode (22 July), Avani Nandra-Hart and Amy Mitchell lure him to a park party, where the group plans to confront him. Ross Marshall, Joel’s father, reluctantly allows his attendance, unaware of their intentions. The twist escalates tensions in Albert Square.

offbeat.title: Charli XCX recrute Vincent Cassel dans le clip de son nouveau single "Camera"
offbeat.summary: Charli XCX’s new single Camera drops July 24 with a noir-and-white clip featuring Vincent Cassel in a chaotic desert shoot. Cassel’s explosive scenes kept failing—first a misfired charge, then a flaming light—until he stormed off, mimicking his iconic La Haine pistol gesture. The track is the final single from her album Music, Fashion, Film.

offbeat.title: PRONOS PARIS RMC Le pari sûr du 22 juillet - Ligue Conférence
offbeat.summary: SC Braga faces FK Železničar Pančevo in the second qualifying round of the UEFA Europa Conference League on July 22 at 21:00 CET. Braga, unbeaten in five pre-season matches, is the clear favorite after opening their domestic league campaign with a win. The Portuguese side must travel to Sportsko-Rekreativni Centar Mladost in Pančevo, Serbia, for the first leg. Braga’s experience and squad depth are expected to secure a positive result ahead of the return fixture.

offbeat.title: Morant: "Feijóo está dibujando una coalición que se parece cada vez más a esos gobiernos criminales como el de Hitler"
offbeat.summary: Spain’s science minister Diana Morant accused PP leader Alberto Núñez Feijóo of steering a potential coalition toward policies resembling Hitler’s regime, citing rising xenophobic rhetoric and racist attacks on footballer Lamine Yamal. The PP demanded an apology and retraction, calling her comparison unacceptable. Morant warned of laws targeting citizens and urged the PP to condemn hate speech. A PP spokesperson called the remarks inflammatory.

offbeat.title: Meteo MARCHE Video: previsioni aggiornate
offbeat.summary: Heavy rain and localized thunderstorms hit the Marche region Friday, prompting a 36-hour yellow weather alert from 2 p.m. on July 11 to midnight on July 12. Intense downpours struck inland and high-hill areas, including Piandimeleto, Mercatale and Peglio in the Pesaro province. Brief coastal and hillside storms are expected overnight Saturday into Sunday, clearing by mid-morning. Conditions will then stabilize with dry, sunny weather through Monday.

offbeat.title: Islande: vague d'indignation dans le pays après le saccage d'un site naturel par un touriste français
offbeat.summary: Un touriste français a détruit un site naturel protégé en Islande en juin avec deux Land Rover, diffusant les dégâts sur les réseaux sociaux. Il a écopé d’une amende de 3 000 euros, mais a repris ses dégradations peu après. Une seconde interpellation a eu lieu peu après. Les autorités locales et les habitants dénoncent ces actes, soulignant l’importance de préserver les écosystèmes fragiles.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts 2026 LIVE RESULTS: Play SUSPENDED during Menzies' match
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies withdrew from the World Matchplay Darts 2026 in Blackpool after suffering high blood pressure during his match against Ross Smith. The Scotsman led 2-1 before collapsing on stage, prompting medical attention and his exit. Play resumed with Gian van Veen and Gerwyn Price among the remaining contenders. The tournament continues with quarter-final spots still up for grabs.

offbeat.title: Alejandro Garnacho transfer news: Aston Villa agree deal with Chelsea for winger
offbeat.summary: Aston Villa have agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until 2027, with a conditional obligation to buy. The 22-year-old Argentina international has already completed a medical and pre-signed a four-year contract at Villa Park. Chelsea expect the buy clause to be triggered, valuing the deal at £43m. Villa have spent nearly £200m on sales this summer.

offbeat.title: Chicago P.D., la tredicesima stagione da stasera su Italia 1: le anticipazioni del 22 luglio
offbeat.summary: Chicago P.D. Season 13 premieres tonight, July 22, at 9:30 p.m. on Italia 1 with 21 new episodes. The gritty police drama follows the Windy City’s Intelligence Unit, led by Sergeant Hank Voight, as they tackle fresh threats after a brutal internal conflict left the team fractured. New undercover agent Eva Imani joins the squad, while old tensions simmer beneath the surface. The season picks up where Season 12 left off, with Voight rebuilding his decimated unit.

offbeat.title: Televisión Canaria presenta ‘Venezuela: un mes bajo los escombros’
offbeat.summary: Televisión Canaria presents a special report Thursday at 21:30 on the aftermath of Venezuela’s June 24 double earthquake. The disaster killed over 5,000 people, injured thousands, and destroyed or severely damaged nearly 18,000 homes. Focused on La Guaira and Caracas, the program marks one month since the quake amid ongoing humanitarian efforts. Survivors’ testimonies highlight the slow recovery in the worst-hit areas.

offbeat.title: Il processo di stasera a "Un giorno in pretura"
offbeat.summary: The final hearing of Giampiero Gualandi, 64, accused of killing Sofia Stefani, 33, concludes tonight in Bologna. The vigilessa was shot with her service pistol on May 16, 2024, at a local police station in Anzola dell’Emilia. The trial, featured on Rai 3’s Un giorno in pretura at 9:15 PM, centers on a stormy relationship marked by a BDSM contract. Prosecutors claim Gualandi acted to silence Stefani over fears she’d expose their affair.

offbeat.title: [Galaxy Unpacked July 2026] A First Look at Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra, Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8
offbeat.summary: Samsung unveiled three new foldable devices at Galaxy Unpacked in London on July 2026. The lineup includes the first Galaxy Z Fold8 Ultra with an 8-inch foldable display and a 4.1mm thin profile. The Galaxy Z Fold8 and Galaxy Z Flip8 also debuted, featuring AI-enhanced multitasking and FlexWindow improvements. Availability and pricing details were not announced.

offbeat.title: Restablecida la circulación de Cercanías en Madrid entre Getafe y Parla tras un incendio junto a la vía
offbeat.summary: Train service on Madrid’s Cercanías line C4 between Getafe Centro and Parla was suspended for about 30 minutes Wednesday after a fire near the tracks. Service resumed at 5:20 p.m. as emergency crews cleared the area. Delays and altered routes affected passengers before normal frequencies gradually returned.

offbeat.title: Death of teen in crash as garda pursued ruled accidental
offbeat.summary: A coroner ruled the 2019 death of 14-year-old Thomas Healy accidental after his car crashed while being pursued by Gardaí in Killarney. The teen lost control on Ross Road, striking a tree; his passenger, 15-year-old Alfie Barrett, survived with severe injuries. Garda Seán O’Sullivan testified he lost sight of the vehicle before the crash, and a 2022 probe cleared him of wrongdoing. The case highlights risks of high-speed pursuits involving young drivers.

offbeat.title: Gratteri bacchetta Salvini sul caso Roggero: “La politica si dia una calmata e la smetta di aizzare la gente”. Su La7
offbeat.summary: Procuratore capo di Napoli Nicola Gratteri ha esortato la politica a evitare di strumentalizzare casi giudiziari, citando le polemiche sugli scontri di Bologna e la condanna del gioielliere Mario Roggero. Gratteri ha sottolineato come entrambi i casi siano stati usati per propaganda elettorale, invitando a rispettare le sentenze definitive. Ha ribadito che la grazia può essere discussa solo dopo l’ingresso in carcere e con prove concrete di ravvedimento. Il magistrato ha chiuso ogni processo mediatico, affidando valutazioni ai soli educatori penitenziari.

offbeat.title: Africa’s most developed pharmaceutical market reviews 12 Ozempic generics as drugmakers target Novo Nordisk’s dominance
offbeat.summary: South Africa’s health regulator is reviewing 12 applications for generic versions of semaglutide, the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic and Wegovy. The move follows the expiry of Novo Nordisk’s main patent in March 2026, opening the door for cheaper alternatives in one of Africa’s largest pharmaceutical markets. Analysts say the approvals could pressure Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly by expanding access to GLP-1 medicines used for diabetes and weight loss. Decisions on the generics are pending, with no timeline announced.

offbeat.title: Cameron Menzies became unwell during his second-round match against Ross Smith
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies’ World Matchplay Darts second-round match against Ross Smith was abandoned Wednesday night after he fell ill mid-game. The Scot was leading 2-1 when he became unsteady and required medical attention. Medics confirmed he was conscious and talking but did not return. Smith advances to the quarter-finals as a result.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts LIVE! Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
offbeat.summary: World Matchplay Darts 2026 runs from July 18 to July 22 in Blackpool, England. Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen are among the top seeds competing. Sky Sports will broadcast the tournament live across its platforms. The event concludes with the final on Wednesday, July 22.

offbeat.title: La Stazione spaziale internazionale attraversa i cieli: passaggio visibile anche nel Bresciano. Ecco quando
offbeat.summary: Tonight at 11:08 PM, the International Space Station will streak across the skies over Brescia, visible to the naked eye for about five minutes. The ISS will appear as a steady, bright light moving from northwest to east-northeast. Clear weather is needed for optimal viewing. Check Heavens-Above for precise local tracking details.

offbeat.title: Après les fortes chaleurs, des orages possiblement violents sont attendus… mais jusqu’à quand va durer la canicule dans la région
offbeat.summary: A prolonged heatwave continues to affect the Languedoc region, with temperatures exceeding 30 degrees Celsius. According to Météo France, the heatwave is expected to last until Saturday, with a potential change in conditions on Saturday. Temperatures are expected to remain high, with a maximum temperature of 35 degrees Celsius forecasted for the region. Residents can expect a return to more typical weather patterns by Sunday.

offbeat.title: „Online-Banking demnächst gesperrt”: Diese Nachrichten sollten Sie direkt an die Sparkasse senden
offbeat.summary: Betrüger senden Mails über angebliches Sicherheitsupdate beim Sparkassen-Banking, um Empfänger auf gefälschte Seiten zu lotsen und Bankdaten abzugreifen. Die Nachricht droht mit einer Sperre des Online-Bankings, falls die angebliche Freischaltung nicht durchgeführt wird. Die Mail ist unpersönlich und der Absender nutzt eine Domain namens ashoj.com. Sparkassen-Kunden sollen sensible Daten preisgeben, indem sie auf eine gefälschte ChipTAN-Seite klicken.

offbeat.title: Gespierde armen, krachtige taal: Van Persie en Steijn beloven 'heel veel goals'
offbeat.summary: Feyenoord's training camp in Belgium features a 32-player squad, including Shaqueel van Persie and Sem Steijn, who have been working out extensively to improve their physique. Van Persie, recovering from a knee injury, aims to become the team's first striker and score many goals. He will need to impress the new coach, Giovanni van Bronckhorst, to achieve his goal. Van Persie's focus remains on his recovery and upcoming season.

offbeat.title: EastEnders kicks off Linda Carter romance story as new character arrives
offbeat.summary: EastEnders has introduced a new character, Clive Masters, played by Jason Durr, who strikes up a friendship with Linda Carter. The two share an instant spark during a salsa dancing lesson at the community centre. Clive's arrival helps Linda in her quest to save the community centre, which is at risk of shutting down. As a result, Elaine decides to play matchmaker for the pair.

offbeat.title: €1.5 billion for tax cuts in Budget - Economic Statement
offbeat.summary: The Irish government's Summer Economic Statement reveals €1.5 billion will be allocated for tax cuts in the upcoming Budget, set for October. This move aims to allow workers to keep more of their pay. Total government expenditure is expected to rise by 5.9% to €125 billion in 2027. The increased spending and tax package will be closely monitored to ensure alignment with the government's Medium Term Framework.

offbeat.title: Toter Wal vom Badestrand entfernt - Teile des Kopfes fehlten
offbeat.summary: A dead pig whale washed up on Norderney's beach has been removed. The carcass was found near a popular bar on Monday and was transported to a facility for disposal. According to Norderney's mayor, Frank Ulrichs, the decision to remove it was made because it was in the area of the public beaches. The whale's head was partially missing.

offbeat.title: The Hawk review – Will Ferrell’s dated golf comedy just isn’t that funny
offbeat.summary: Will Ferrell stars in a new Netflix show, "The Hawk," a golf comedy that revisits his broad, dated style of humor. The 59-year-old actor, a key figure in the 2000s comedy scene, has stuck to this approach despite the shift in popular taste. The show, released on Netflix, marks a departure from the more nuanced comedy of his peers. Ferrell's decision to revive his signature style has sparked questions about whether it's time for a comeback.

offbeat.title: Tennis: Ana Ivanović macht ein trauriges Geständnis
offbeat.summary: Former tennis star Ana Ivanović has revealed she ended her career early due to mental health issues, in addition to physical injuries. The 38-year-old, who won the 2008 French Open and reached number one in the world, spoke about her struggles in a recent interview. Ivanović said she experienced severe panic attacks on court, which she had never publicly discussed before. Her decision to retire at 29 in 2016 now takes on a more complex context.

offbeat.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
offbeat.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2024 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother-of-four Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Prosecutors say the woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. She has now been formally charged, with the investigating judge to decide if there's enough evidence for a trial. The case was opened on May 7, 2025.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant in response to ongoing attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The potential targets include those located near the capital Tehran. This warning follows 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran. Iran has warned of reprisals after Trump said the US would soon hit an underground nuclear site near Natanz.

offbeat.title: It's Officially the End of an Era for Marvel's 'Wonder Man'
offbeat.summary: Marvel Studios has officially canceled its Wonder Man Disney+ series, marking the end of an era for the character. The show was set to premiere in 2024, but production has been halted indefinitely. The decision comes after a year of development, with several cast members and crew members already attached to the project. The future of the character remains uncertain, but fans are left wondering what this means for the Marvel Cinematic Universe.

offbeat.title: « L’Agence : nouvelles destinations » est de retour sur TMC, que réserve la saison 2 ?
offbeat.summary: The reality TV show "L'Agence : nouvelles destinations" returns to TMC for its second season, with Martin and Valentin Kretz competing to find new destinations for their family's luxury real estate agency. The brothers, members of a family of high-end agents, aim to expand their business by discovering new markets. The competition, which drew nearly 500,000 viewers per episode in 2025, resumes on July 22, 2026. The stakes are high as the brothers vie for the best destination.

offbeat.title: RETE 4
offbeat.summary: A 91-year-old Illinois florist, Earl Stone, has turned to transporting large quantities of drugs to make ends meet after facing economic crisis. This marks a drastic change for the veteran war convert who once prioritized moral principles. The film, directed by Clint Eastwood, is based on a true story and explores themes of regret and redemption. As the film airs tonight at 9:35 PM, viewers can expect a poignant portrayal of an American icon's downfall.

offbeat.title: Fast Sylvester Stallone getötet: Dolph Lundgrens Reaktion auf den schlimmen Action-Vorfall bei „Rocky“
offbeat.summary: Sylvester Stallone was hospitalized in 1985 after Dolph Lundgren knocked him out during filming of Rocky IV. The incident occurred during a real boxing scene when Lundgren landed a powerful punch. Stallone suffered a concussion and required medical treatment. The film’s intense fight choreography reflected the era before advanced digital effects.

offbeat.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
offbeat.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, was found dead at his home in Colombes, a Paris suburb, on Monday. An investigation into his death has been opened, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad's name appeared thousands of times in Epstein files, and he was under investigation for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges. The investigation into his death and the ongoing probe into human trafficking charges will continue.

tech.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
tech.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted to $1,549.99 at Amazon and $1,549 at Best Buy, down from its usual $1,699 price. This premium iPad features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. Additionally, the 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage and optional 5G is marked down to $749, a $500 discount. Consumers can now purchase these devices at lower prices.

tech.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
tech.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to introduce a new financing program for leasing iPhones, which could allow the company to restrict access to apps on devices with missed payments. Under the program, iPhones in "Restricted Mode" would lose access to nearly all apps, except for basic functions. The feature is part of iOS 27 beta, which is still in development. If implemented, it could impact users who miss payments on their financed devices.

tech.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
tech.summary: A Florida teenager, 15-year-old R.K.C., has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial and a desire to focus on his recovery and therapy. The lawsuit claimed Meta's platforms, including Instagram, were addictive and harmed children. R.K.C. had previously settled similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. Meta has defended itself against the lawsuit, stating the claims were baseless.

entertainment.title: ‘Disney Worldbuilders’ Documentary by Leslie Iwerks, Featuring James Cameron, Kevin Feige and Jon Favreau, Set to Premiere on Disney+
entertainment.summary: A new documentary, "Disney Worldbuilders," is set to premiere on Disney+ on August 20. The film, directed by Leslie Iwerks, takes viewers behind the scenes of Disney's immersive park experiences, including franchises like "Frozen" and "Zootopia." Featuring interviews with James Cameron, Kevin Feige, and Jon Favreau, the documentary showcases the creation of Disney's park worlds. The premiere marks a unique look at the intersection of film and theme park design.

entertainment.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
entertainment.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing they can "weirdly take you out of reality." This ban was evident on his recent film "The Odyssey," where actors wore traditional Ancient Greek attire. Nolan's reasoning is that Uggs are a "signifier of modernity" that can detract from the film's historical setting. The ban is likely to continue on future sets, ensuring a more immersive experience for audiences.

entertainment.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
entertainment.summary: Bose has launched the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, a flagship wireless speaker in its reimagined Lifestyle Collection, priced at $299. The speaker is currently a bestseller on Best Buy and is available in two colorways. It can be expanded into a multi-room setup or a full home theater over time. The Lifestyle Ultra Speaker is part of a new lineup that includes a soundbar and subwoofer, available in an exclusive bundle on Best Buy for $1,699.

politics.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
politics.summary: Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has sparked a political row by ordering a review of plans to release thousands of violent offenders from prison in September. Approximately 6,000 killers, rapists, and sex offenders are set to be freed on tags. The move comes after the family of PC Andrew Harper expressed outrage over the potential early release of his killers. The review aims to address concerns over public safety and the criminal justice system.

politics.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
politics.summary: President Trump's tariffs have reshaped America's trading relationship with the world, with imports from countries like China falling by nearly 40% since tariffs went into effect. As a result, manufacturers have sought low-cost factories closer to home, prompting a boom in trade between the United States and Mexico. Laredo, Texas, has been a major beneficiary, with roughly 40% of U.S.-Mexico trade passing through the city. The city's World Trade Bridge is now a key hub for Mexican products entering the US.

politics.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
politics.summary: The UK's Conservative Party has asked HMRC to investigate a £5m gift to Nigel Farage, questioning whether it should be taxed as compensation for his failed bid to become an MP. The gift was given by crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne, who claims it was a personal and unconditional donation. Farage has previously stated the money was for personal security costs and a Brexit campaign reward. HMRC will now review the matter to determine if tax is owed.

breaking.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
breaking.summary: Adolf Hitler's birthplace in Braunau am Inn, Austria, has been converted into a police station, marking the end of a yearslong effort to prevent the site from becoming a Nazi attraction. The 17th century building, located at Salzburger Vorstadt 15, has undergone renovations for about three years. The police station is now operational, with a new exterior and windows. This move comes as Austria continues to grapple with its role in the Holocaust.

breaking.title: Dubai Firm Cuts Deal for New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
breaking.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

breaking.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
breaking.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

crisis.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
crisis.summary: Global oil prices surged to a 14-month high on Wednesday, driven by escalating tensions between the US and Iran. The US has threatened to deepen its war effort, including targeting the Houthis, and warned Iran that attacking any ship in the Strait of Hormuz would prompt a US bombing of a bridge or power plant in Tehran. The conflict has already led to a significant decline in shipping traffic in the region. The situation remains volatile, with the US continuing to attack Iran.

crisis.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
crisis.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

crisis.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
crisis.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

sports.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
sports.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m if certain conditions are met. The 22-year-old has already completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. This deal follows Chelsea's signing of Morgan Rogers for a record £117m fee from Villa on Tuesday. Garnacho's loan is expected to be triggered if he meets certain performance conditions next season.

sports.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been handed a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The penalty, awarded to Celtic in a 3-2 win at Motherwell, kept Celtic one point behind Hearts, who ultimately won the title. McInnes is due to serve the ban, with one game suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership season.

sports.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
sports.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation centers on discovery rights, an MLS regulation that gives a specific club first refusal over a player. Inter Miami acquired Casemiro's rights from the LA Galaxy, but the Galaxy held those rights initially. The league will review the case before making further comments.

facts.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
facts.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility is the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years. The event will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and a media availability with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials. The wind tunnel is a major milestone in NASA's evolution, marking a significant step forward in the agency's research capabilities.

facts.title: 1,700 H.I.V. Treatment Sites Closed After Trump Aid Cuts, a Study Finds
facts.summary: A new study reveals that 1,700 H.I.V. treatment sites worldwide have closed following the Trump administration's cuts to the largest global H.I.V. program last year. The cuts led to significant drops in prevention efforts and treatment for high-risk groups, including sex workers, men who have sex with men, and people who inject drugs. The study, conducted by the H.I.V. charity amfAR, found that three-quarters of affected organizations stopped providing services to these groups. The findings will be presented next week at an international AIDS conference in Rio de Janeiro.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report finds that ultra-processed foods are a primary driver of obesity and type 2 diabetes. The report suggests that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed, as evidence points to sugar and refined carbohydrates as the main culprits. The committee recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to hold them accountable for healthier food options. This could lead to a re-evaluation of food labelling and reformulation targets.

health.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
health.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City amid a legionnaires' disease outbreak on Manhattan's Upper East Side, with 82 cases identified and 8 people still hospitalized. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings that tested positive for live legionella bacteria. Testing is ongoing, with results for 140 remaining cooling tower systems expected in the coming week. The city's health department will continue to monitor the situation.

health.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
health.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
